title: Punishment for Murder
summary: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
punishment: Death or Life Imprisonment + Fine

title: Punishment for Culpable Homicide not amounting to Murder
summary: Culpable homicide not amounting to murder is punishable. If the act is done with intention, imprisonment up to 10 years. If without intention, up to 10 years or fine or both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years or Fine or Both

title: Attempt to Murder
summary: Whoever does any act with intention or knowledge to cause death shall be punished with imprisonment up to 10 years and fine. If hurt is caused, imprisonment may extend to life.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years + Fine; Life imprisonment if hurt caused

title: Assault or Criminal Force on Woman with Intent to Outrage her Modesty
summary: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force on any woman intending to outrage or knowing it likely to outrage her modesty shall be punished.
punishment: Imprisonment 1 to 5 years + Fine

title: Rape
summary: A man is said to commit rape if he has sexual intercourse with a woman against her will, without her consent, or under coercion, fraud, or intoxication.
punishment: Rigorous Imprisonment not less than 7 years, may extend to Life + Fine

title: Punishment for Rape
summary: Whoever commits rape shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment of not less than 10 years, extendable to life imprisonment, and fine.
punishment: Rigorous Imprisonment minimum 10 years to Life + Fine

title: Punishment for Theft
summary: Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or Fine or Both

title: Theft in Dwelling House
summary: Whoever commits theft in any building, tent, or vessel used as a human dwelling shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years + Fine

title: Extortion
summary: Whoever intentionally puts a person in fear of injury or harm and thereby dishonestly induces that person to deliver property commits extortion.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or Fine or Both

title: Robbery
summary: In all robbery there is either theft or extortion. Theft is robbery when force or threat of force is used in commission of the theft.
punishment: Rigorous Imprisonment up to 10 years + Fine

title: Punishment for Dacoity
summary: Whoever commits dacoity (robbery committed by 5 or more persons) shall be punished with imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine.
punishment: Life Imprisonment or Rigorous Imprisonment up to 10 years + Fine

title: Criminal Breach of Trust
summary: Whoever being entrusted with property dishonestly misappropriates or converts it for his own use commits criminal breach of trust.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or Fine or Both

title: Cheating
summary: Whoever by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces that person to deliver any property or to do or omit to do anything commits cheating.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or Fine or Both

title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
summary: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person to deliver any property, or to make or alter valuable security commits an offence under section 420.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years + Fine

title: Husband or Relative of Husband of a Woman Subjecting her to Cruelty
summary: Whoever being the husband or relative of the husband of a woman subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished. Cruelty includes wilful conduct causing grave injury and harassment for dowry.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years + Fine

title: Dowry Death
summary: Where the death of a woman is caused by burns, bodily injury or occurs under suspicious circumstances within 7 years of marriage and it is shown she was subjected to cruelty for dowry, it is dowry death.
punishment: Imprisonment not less than 7 years, may extend to Life

title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt
summary: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt to any person shall be punished with imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or Fine up to Rs. 1000 or Both

title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt by Dangerous Weapons
summary: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing, cutting, or by fire, heated substance, poison or explosive shall be punished.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or Fine or Both

title: Word, Gesture or Act Intended to Insult the Modesty of a Woman
summary: Whoever intending to insult the modesty of any woman utters any word, makes any sound or gesture, or exhibits any object shall be punished.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years + Fine

title: Criminal Intimidation
summary: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property, or to the person or reputation of any one in whom that person is interested, to cause alarm or to do an act commits criminal intimidation.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or Fine or Both

title: Definition of Consumer
summary: A consumer is any person who buys goods or avails services for consideration. It does not include a person who obtains goods for resale or commercial purpose.
punishment: N/A (Definitional provision)

title: Jurisdiction of District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
summary: District Consumer Commission handles complaints where value of goods/services + compensation claimed does not exceed Rs. 1 crore.
punishment: Compensation + Refund as ordered by Commission

title: Manner of Filing Complaint
summary: A complaint can be filed by the consumer, consumer association, Central/State Government, or one or more consumers on behalf of many. Complaint can be filed online on edaakhil.nic.in.
punishment: N/A (Procedural provision)

title: Jurisdiction of State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
summary: State Consumer Commission handles complaints where value exceeds Rs. 1 crore but does not exceed Rs. 10 crores.
punishment: Compensation as ordered by State Commission

title: Jurisdiction of National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
summary: National Consumer Commission handles complaints where value exceeds Rs. 10 crores. Also handles appeals from State Commissions.
punishment: Compensation as ordered by National Commission

title: Unfair Trade Practice
summary: Unfair trade practice means a trade practice which for the purpose of promoting sale adopts unfair method or deceptive practice including false representation of quality, quantity, grade, standard of goods/services.
punishment: Refund + Compensation + Penalty up to Rs. 10 lakhs

title: Penalty for Manufacture of Spurious Goods
summary: If a person manufactures or sells spurious goods which cause grievous hurt or death, they are liable for imprisonment and fine.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years + Fine up to Rs. 5 lakhs (death: Life imprisonment)

title: Deficiency in Service
summary: Deficiency means any fault, imperfection, shortcoming or inadequacy in the quality, nature and manner of performance which is required to be maintained by or under any law for the time being in force.
punishment: Refund + Compensation as ordered by Consumer Commission

title: E-Commerce Consumer Rights
summary: E-commerce entities must display all mandatory information, provide return/refund mechanism, and cannot manipulate prices. Consumer has right to return goods and get full refund.
punishment: Penalty as per Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules 2020

title: Duration of Lease in Absence of Written Contract
summary: In absence of a written contract, a lease of immovable property for agricultural purposes is from year to year, and for any other purpose, it is from month to month. Either party must give 15 days notice to terminate.
punishment: N/A (Civil provision — tenant can approach Civil Court)

title: Rights and Liabilities of Lessor and Lessee
summary: Landlord must disclose material defects in property. Tenant has right to undisturbed possession. Tenant must pay rent on time, keep property in good condition and not sublet without permission.
punishment: N/A (Civil provision — approach Civil Court for disputes)

title: Protection Against Eviction
summary: A tenant cannot be evicted without valid legal grounds. Valid grounds include non-payment of rent, subletting without permission, misuse of premises, or landlord needing property for personal use. Landlord must give notice and approach Rent Controller.
punishment: Illegal eviction: Landlord liable under IPC 441 (Criminal Trespass) + Tenant can claim damages

title: Compulsory Registration of Rent Agreement
summary: Lease/rent agreements for more than 11 months MUST be registered. 11-month agreements are popular because they avoid compulsory registration. Unregistered agreements above 11 months are not admissible as evidence in court.
punishment: Unregistered lease is inadmissible in court as evidence

title: Obligations of Promoter Regarding Veracity of Advertisement
summary: If a builder makes a false statement in advertisement or prospectus, the buyer can withdraw from the project and get full refund with interest. Builder is liable for all losses.
punishment: Refund + Interest + Compensation; Imprisonment up to 3 years for repeated violations

title: Return of Amount and Compensation on Delayed Possession
summary: If builder fails to complete construction or give possession by agreed date, buyer can withdraw and claim full refund with interest, or continue and claim interest for every month of delay.
punishment: Full refund + Interest at SBI MCLR + 2% for delay period

title: Criminal Trespass
summary: Whoever enters into or upon property in possession of another with intent to commit offence or to intimidate, insult or annoy any person in possession of such property commits criminal trespass.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or Fine up to Rs. 500 or Both

title: Prohibition of Benami Transactions
summary: No person shall enter into any benami transaction. Property held under benami transaction is liable to be confiscated by the Government.
punishment: Rigorous Imprisonment 1 to 7 years + Fine up to 25% of fair market value of property

title: Divorce
summary: Grounds for divorce include: adultery, cruelty, desertion for 2+ years, conversion of religion, unsoundness of mind, leprosy, venereal disease, renunciation of world, or presumption of death.
punishment: N/A (Civil provision — petition in Family Court)

title: Divorce by Mutual Consent
summary: Both husband and wife can file for divorce by mutual consent if they have been living separately for 1 year or more. Court grants divorce after 6-18 months cooling off period.
punishment: N/A (Civil provision — 6 to 18 months process in Family Court)

title: Maintenance Pendente Lite and Expenses of Proceedings
summary: Either spouse can claim maintenance during pendency of matrimonial proceedings. Court may order payment of expenses and monthly sum as it deems just.
punishment: N/A (Civil provision — court orders maintenance amount)

title: Maintenance of Wife
summary: A Hindu wife is entitled to be maintained by her husband during her lifetime. She can claim maintenance if husband deserts her, treats her with cruelty, is suffering from virulent leprosy or keeps a concubine.
punishment: N/A (Civil provision — wife can approach Family Court)

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005
title: Definition of Domestic Violence
summary: Domestic violence includes physical, sexual, verbal, emotional and economic abuse by husband, family members or live-in partner against a woman.
punishment: Protection Order + Residence Order + Monetary Relief + Compensation

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005
title: Application to Magistrate
summary: An aggrieved woman, Protection Officer or any person on behalf of aggrieved can file application to Magistrate for relief under Domestic Violence Act.
punishment: Protection Order + Residence rights + Monetary compensation up to Rs. 20 lakhs

title: Devolution of Interest in Coparcenary Property
summary: After 2005 amendment, daughters have equal coparcenary rights in ancestral Hindu Undivided Family property, same as sons. Daughter's right exists even if father died before 2005.
punishment: N/A (Civil provision — approach Civil Court for partition)

title: Conditions for Marriage under Special Marriage Act
summary: Any two persons irrespective of religion can marry under Special Marriage Act. Conditions: neither party has living spouse, of sound mind, male 21 years, female 18 years.
punishment: N/A (Civil provision — register at Marriage Registrar's office)

title: Matters to be Considered by Court Appointing Guardian
summary: In matters of child custody, the welfare of the child is the paramount consideration. Court considers age, sex of child, wishes of parents, personal law of parents and child's own wishes if old enough.
punishment: N/A (Civil provision — Family Court decides custody)

title: Penalty for Giving or Taking Dowry
summary: Giving or taking dowry is prohibited. Any property given as dowry belongs to the bride. Demanding dowry is a criminal offence.
punishment: Imprisonment minimum 5 years + Fine minimum Rs. 15,000 or value of dowry (whichever higher)

act: Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Marriage) Act 2019
title: Triple Talaq Void and Illegal
summary: Any pronouncement of talaq by a Muslim husband upon his wife, by words spoken or written or in electronic form, shall be void and illegal.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years + Fine

title: Penalty for Damage to Computer System
summary: If any person without permission accesses or downloads data from another's computer system, introduces virus, or causes damage, they are liable to pay compensation up to Rs. 1 crore.
punishment: Compensation up to Rs. 1 crore to affected party

title: Computer Related Offences
summary: Whoever commits any act referred to in Section 43 dishonestly or fraudulently commits a computer related offence and shall be punished.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or Fine up to Rs. 5 lakhs or Both

title: Identity Theft
summary: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly makes use of the electronic signature, password or any other unique identification feature of any other person commits identity theft.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years + Fine up to Rs. 1 lakh

title: Cheating by Personation Using Computer Resource
summary: Whoever cheats by personation using communication device or computer resource (fake websites, UPI fraud, phishing) commits an offence.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years + Fine up to Rs. 1 lakh

title: Violation of Privacy
summary: Whoever intentionally captures, publishes or transmits image of private area of a person without their consent violates their privacy.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or Fine up to Rs. 2 lakhs or Both

title: Publishing Obscene Material in Electronic Form
summary: Whoever publishes or transmits obscene material in electronic form shall be punished.
punishment: First conviction: Imprisonment up to 3 years + Fine up to Rs. 5 lakhs; Subsequent: up to 5 years + Rs. 10 lakhs

title: Publishing Sexually Explicit Material in Electronic Form
summary: Whoever publishes or transmits material containing sexually explicit act or conduct in electronic form shall be punished.
punishment: First conviction: Imprisonment up to 5 years + Fine up to Rs. 10 lakhs

title: Publishing Child Sexually Abusive Material
summary: Whoever publishes, transmits or browses child sexually abusive material in electronic form shall be punished.
punishment: First conviction: Imprisonment up to 5 years + Fine up to Rs. 10 lakhs; Subsequent: up to 7 years + Rs. 10 lakhs

title: Power to Block Public Access of Information
summary: Central Government may block access to any information generated, transmitted, received, stored in any computer resource in interest of sovereignty, integrity, defence or public order.
punishment: Non-compliance: Imprisonment up to 7 years + Fine

title: Breach of Confidentiality and Privacy
summary: If any person having secured access to electronic record, book, register, document, or information discloses such material to any other person, they commit breach of confidentiality.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or Fine up to Rs. 1 lakh or Both

title: Cyber Stalking and Online Harassment
summary: Sending threatening, abusive, or harassing messages online or repeatedly following/contacting someone online without consent is cyber stalking and punishable under IPC and IT Act.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years + Fine

title: Promoting Enmity Between Groups
summary: Whoever promotes disharmony or feelings of enmity, hatred or ill-will between different religious, racial, language or regional groups by words, signs or visible representations commits an offence.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or Fine or Both

title: Defamation
summary: Whoever by words spoken or intended to be read, by signs, or visible representations makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person intending to harm their reputation commits defamation.
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or Fine or Both
